editorsNote: Today's landscape is dominated by a massive shift in AI safety and cybersecurity, led by Anthropic's 'Project Glasswing' and a significant leak of their 'Claude Code' source code. Meanwhile, the healthcare sector is seeing a surge in autonomous clinical pilots, with Utah leading a controversial program for AI-driven prescription renewals.

healthcareStories.headline: Utah Launches Autonomous AI Prescription Pilot
healthcareStories.summary:
The state of Utah has initiated a first-of-its-kind pilot program allowing an AI system from Doctronic to autonomously issue prescription renewals. The program, overseen by the Utah Office of Artificial Intelligence Policy (OAIP), operates in three phases. Initially, every renewal requires a physician's sign-off. By the final phase, the AI will handle 90-95% of renewals independently, with physicians reviewing only a small sample. 

This move has sparked significant pushback from the American Medical Association (AMA) and the Utah Academy of Family Physicians. Critics argue that removing physicians from the clinical loop poses severe patient risks and bypasses FDA medical device regulations. However, proponents point to the program's strict guardrails: it excludes controlled substances and injectables, and the AI is held to the same malpractice standards as a human physician. 

Why it matters: This represents a major regulatory shift toward 'autonomous' AI in clinical practice. If successful, it could drastically reduce physician administrative burden and wait times for routine medications, but it sets a precedent for state-level deregulation that may clash with federal FDA oversight.

healthcareStories.headline: JAMA Study: AI Scribes Boost Revenue and Reduce Burnout
healthcareStories.summary:
A new study published in JAMA reveals that the adoption of AI-powered clinical scribes is delivering measurable efficiency gains for healthcare providers. On average, clinicians using AI scribes saved 13 minutes per day on Electronic Health Record (EHR) tasks and 16 minutes on documentation. For primary care providers, the impact was even greater, with a reduction of 25 minutes in daily EHR time.

Beyond time savings, the study found a 1.7% increase in weekly patient visit volume, translating to an average monthly revenue increase of $167 per clinician. The research highlights that the most significant benefits were seen by female providers and those who used the tools in at least 50% of their patient encounters.

Why it matters: While the time savings per day may seem modest, the cumulative effect on physician burnout and the direct impact on the bottom line provide a strong business case for health systems to scale ambient AI technologies.

healthcareStories.headline: Rune Labs Launches Parkinson's AI Companion via Claude
healthcareStories.summary:
Rune Labs has announced 'StrivePD Guardian,' a personalized AI companion for patients with Parkinson's disease. The tool is powered by Anthropic's Claude and trained on Rune Labs' massive longitudinal dataset, which includes millions of hours of wearable data and FDA-cleared symptom tracking. 

The companion provides real-time, context-aware guidance on medication timing, symptom management, and daily activities by analyzing data from the patient's wearable devices. It aims to bridge the gap between clinical visits by providing patients with continuous support based on their specific physiological data.

Why it matters: This is a prime example of 'Vertical AI' in healthcare, where a general-purpose LLM (Claude) is combined with highly specialized, proprietary medical data to create a high-value clinical application.

techStories.headline: Anthropic Unveils Project Glasswing to Fix Zero-Days
techStories.summary:
Anthropic has launched 'Project Glasswing,' a defensive cybersecurity initiative powered by a new, unreleased model called 'Claude Mythos Preview.' The company claims Mythos is so adept at finding software vulnerabilities that it has already identified thousands of zero-day flaws across every major operating system and web browser. 

Due to the model's 'unprecedented' capability to exploit these same weaknesses, Anthropic is withholding a public release. Instead, they have formed a defensive alliance with tech giants including Apple, Google, Microsoft, NVIDIA, and CrowdStrike. These partners are using Mythos to find and patch vulnerabilities before the model's capabilities inevitably proliferate to bad actors. 

Why it matters: This marks a new era of 'AI-driven cyber-warfare' where the same model can be the ultimate lockpick or the ultimate locksmith. Anthropic's decision to restrict access highlights the growing tension between open-source progress and national security risks.

techStories.headline: Massive Leak: Claude Code Source Code Exposed
techStories.summary:
In a major security embarrassment, the full source code for Anthropic's 'Claude Code' CLI tool was leaked via an exposed source map file on the npm registry. The leak, discovered by researcher Chaofan Shou, revealed over 500,000 lines of TypeScript and exposed several unreleased features, including 'Undercover Mode' for stealth contributions and internal-only employee prompts.

Analysis of the leaked code suggests that Anthropic uses specific system instructions for its own employees—such as 'verify work actually works before claiming done'—that are not present in the public version. The leak also revealed a flag for 'Anti-Distillation,' a technique designed to poison the training data of competitors who might try to scrape Claude's outputs.

Why it matters: This leak provides a rare 'under the hood' look at how leading AI labs build agentic tools. It also raises questions about the 'safety' branding of companies that suffer basic configuration errors leading to massive IP exposure.

techStories.headline: OpenAI Proposes 'AI New Deal' Amid $122B Funding
techStories.summary:
OpenAI has released a comprehensive policy whitepaper titled 'Industrial Policy for the Intelligence Age,' calling for a 'Roosevelt-style New Deal' for AI. The proposal includes radical ideas such as a public wealth fund to share AI profits, a 'robot tax' to fund social safety nets, and a transition to a 4-day workweek as productivity gains materialize.

The paper was released alongside news that OpenAI has closed a staggering $122 billion funding round, valuing the company at $852 billion. The capital is earmarked for massive compute expansion and the development of 'superintelligence.' However, the proposal has met with skepticism, with some critics labeling it 'regulatory capture' designed to favor incumbents over open-source competitors.

Why it matters: OpenAI is positioning itself not just as a tech company, but as a quasi-governmental architect of the future economy. The sheer scale of their funding suggests they have the capital to influence global policy as much as technology.

socialHighlights.content: Reiterated that language-based reasoning is a 'dead end' for AGI. Argues that true thinking happens in abstract representation spaces, not text. Challenged xAI to adopt the JEPA (Joint-Embedding Predictive Architecture) model if they want to move beyond 'stochastic parrots.'

Introduced 'autoresearch,' a pattern where AI agents are programmed to autonomously run their own training experiments overnight. 'You're not programming the model; you're programming the agent's instructions in a Markdown file.' Claims this is the next layer of Software 2.0.

Warned against 'alarmist narratives' used for regulatory capture. Cited a study showing that 'human extinction' claims are losing public resonance, while concerns about job loss and AI in warfare are rising. Urged for evidence-based policy that targets specific harms rather than broad development.

googlePocItems.title: Serverless RAG Engine with Metadata Filtering
googlePocItems.description: Build a fully managed Retrieval-Augmented Generation (RAG) system that uses the new Vertex AI RAG Engine Serverless mode for automatic scaling and metadata-based search.

guide.instruction: Create a new RAG corpus in Vertex AI using the serverless deployment mode. This avoids the need to provision a dedicated Spanner instance.
guide.codeSnippet:
from google.cloud import aiplatform_v1beta1 as aiplatform

client = aiplatform.RagServiceClient()
corpus = client.create_rag_corpus(
    parent="projects/my-project/locations/us-central1",
    rag_corpus=aiplatform.RagCorpus(display_name="Medical_Docs", deployment_mode="SERVERLESS")
)

guide.instruction: Import documents into the corpus, attaching schema-based metadata (e.g., 'department', 'doc_type') to enable granular filtering during retrieval.
Perform a retrieval query using Gemini, applying a metadata filter to only search within specific document types.

googlePocItems.title: On-Device Multimodal Agent with Gemma 4
googlePocItems.description: Deploy a local AI agent on an Android device using Gemma 4 Effective 2B to perform real-time image and audio analysis without cloud connectivity.

guide.instruction: Convert the Gemma 4 Effective 2B model to a 4-bit quantized TFLite format to fit within mobile memory constraints.
guide.codeSnippet:
import tensorflow as tf
converter = tf.lite.TFLiteConverter.from_keras_model(gemma_model)
converter.optimizations = [tf.lite.Optimize.DEFAULT]
tflite_model = converter.convert()

guide.instruction: Use the MediaPipe LLM Inference API to load the model on the device and handle multimodal inputs (text + image).
Leverage Gemma 4's native function calling to allow the agent to interact with local device APIs (e.g., 'get_location', 'set_alarm').
guide.codeSnippet:
String prompt = "Look at this photo. If it's a pharmacy, find the nearest one to me.";
llmInference.generateResponse(prompt, imageFrame);

deepLearningSpotlight.title: Test-Time Training (TTT) for Infinite Context
deepLearningSpotlight.summary:
The latest edition of 'The Batch' highlights a breakthrough in handling long-context windows called Test-Time Training End-to-End (TTT-E2E). Traditional LLMs suffer from quadratic complexity—as the input gets longer, the model gets exponentially slower and less accurate. TTT-E2E solves this by treating the hidden state of the model as a set of weights that are updated during inference. 

Technically, the model 'learns' from the input it is currently processing. This allows the context window to expand virtually without limit while keeping inference time and memory usage constant. Andrew Ng notes that this could be the 'missing link' for truly agentic systems that need to remember months of interaction history without the massive overhead of current RAG or long-context architectures. He emphasizes that while this is currently a research breakthrough, its implementation in production models like Gemini or Claude could redefine how we build 'always-on' AI assistants.
deepLearningSpotlight.author: Andrew Ng

deepLearningSpotlight.title: The Death of the Video Generation Hype?
deepLearningSpotlight.summary:
In a surprising editorial, Andrew Ng discusses OpenAI's decision to shut down Sora, its high-profile video generation model. Despite the initial viral success, OpenAI cited the 'unsustainable' $1 million-per-day operational cost and a strategic pivot toward 'productivity-driven' tools. Ng argues that this reflects a broader industry trend: the 'Sora-style' brute-force scaling of video is hitting a wall of economic reality. 

Instead, the focus is shifting toward 'Spatial AI'—models that understand 3D physics and can be used in robotics, rather than just creating pretty pixels. Ng suggests that the 'video generation bubble' is bursting, but it is clearing the way for more meaningful applications in manufacturing and physical automation. He views this as a healthy correction that moves the industry from 'entertainment AI' to 'utility AI.'
deepLearningSpotlight.author: Andrew Ng

generalLearningItems.title: Model Context Protocol (MCP) Course
generalLearningItems.summary: A new course teaching developers how to use the Model Context Protocol to give AI agents standardized access to local data, tools, and APIs without custom integration code.

generalLearningItems.title: Claude Agent SDK Guide
generalLearningItems.summary: A comprehensive technical guide and cookbook for building autonomous agents that can manage sessions, handle tool handoffs, and perform multi-step reasoning using the new Claude 4.6 API.

generalLearningItems.title: Harvard: AI for Web Development (Free)
generalLearningItems.summary: A series of six free online courses launched by Harvard University focusing on integrating generative AI into modern web development workflows, specifically for Python and JavaScript developers.
